name: Sliver
categoryDescription: Commonly abused tools that leave NO Windows registry artifacts. These tools are typically single executables, portable builds, or in-memory frameworks. Detection depends entirely on non-registry evidence: process execution logs, prefetch, ShimCache/Amcache, BAM/DAM, network traffic, and memory forensics. Includes detection notes for each tool.
description: Open-source adversary simulation framework (alternative to Cobalt Strike). Go-based implants run in memory. No native registry entries unless persistence is explicitly configured by operator.
forensic_value: No registry artifacts. Go binary characteristics detectable in memory. Sliver generates unique TLS certificates — JA3/JA3S fingerprinting useful.
